<> ```php function replaceUrlInString($input, $oldUrl, $newUrl) { $pattern = '/(' . preg_quote($oldUrl, '/') . ')/'; return preg_replace($pattern, $newUrl, $input); } // Example usage $input = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $oldUrl = "http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1"; $newUrl = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $result = replaceUrlInString($input, $oldUrl, $newUrl); echo $result; ``` This code defines a function `replaceUrlInString` that takes an input string, an old URL, and a new URL. It uses regular expressions to find and replace all occurrences of the old URL with the new URL. The function is designed to handle repeating strings as specified.
